description: Specification for the self-improving Rule Discovery Engine. Defines how new patterns are detected, scored, and promoted into the rule library.

new_rule_candidates.source_doc_id: string — doc_id of the document where pattern was found
new_rule_candidates.detected_pattern: string — the regex or phrase pattern
new_rule_candidates.pattern_type: trigger_verb | actor | deadline | domain_keyword | cross_reference | clause_structure

suggested_rule.id: string — suggested rule ID e.g. TV-041
suggested_rule.obligation_type: mandatory | discretionary | conditional | time_bound | prohibition | approval_required | recommendation

new_rule_candidates.occurrence_count: number — how many times this pattern appeared in the corpus
new_rule_candidates.reasoning: string — why this pattern is worth promoting to a rule
new_rule_candidates.recommended_file: string — which rule file this should be added to e.g. rules/common/trigger_verbs.json

member_patterns: array of similar patterns found in corpus

detection_algorithms.name: novel_trigger_verb_detection
detection_algorithms.description: Find verb phrases that appear before an obligation-like clause but are not in trigger_verbs.json.
detection_algorithms.method: Extract subject-verb-object triples. Compare verbs against known trigger_verbs.json. Score unseen verbs by co-occurrence with known obligation markers.

detection_algorithms.name: novel_actor_detection
detection_algorithms.description: Find noun phrases that appear as subjects in shall/must clauses but are not in actor_patterns.json.
detection_algorithms.method: Extract NPs before 'shall' or 'must'. Compare against actor_patterns.json. Flag unseen entities.

detection_algorithms.name: novel_deadline_expression
detection_algorithms.description: Find temporal expressions not covered by deadline_patterns.json.
detection_algorithms.method: Extract all temporal NPs (regex: \\bwithin|before|by|not later than|not exceeding). Compare against known deadline_patterns.json. Flag novel patterns.

detection_algorithms.name: novel_domain_keyword
detection_algorithms.description: Find technical terms that appear frequently in a semantic cluster but are absent from domain_keywords.json.
detection_algorithms.method: TF-IDF over extracted clauses. Cluster by domain (if domain is already assigned). Flag high-TF-IDF terms not in keyword list.

detection_algorithms.name: novel_cross_reference
detection_algorithms.description: Find references to new circulars, acts, or directions not in cross_reference_patterns.json.
detection_algorithms.method: Regex for formal citation patterns: 'Circular No.', 'Direction', 'Act, 20XX'. Compare against known ref_type list.

detection_algorithms.name: obligation_cluster_similarity
detection_algorithms.description: Cluster obligations by action text. Find obligation types that repeat across documents — candidates for new sub_type rules.
detection_algorithms.method: Sentence embedding similarity (cosine > 0.85). Group and count. Propose new sub_type if cluster size > 10.

detection_algorithms.name: sector_vocabulary_expansion
detection_algorithms.description: Detect domain-specific vocabulary in sector-specific circulars (NBFC, FEMA, Payments) not in sector rule packs.
detection_algorithms.method: Compare extracted keywords against sector_rules/*.json keyword lists. Score by frequency and domain co-occurrence.

stages.name: detection
stages.description: Run detection algorithms on processed documents. Populate new_rule_candidates with status=candidate.

stages.name: llm_review
stages.description: Send candidate rules to LLM for validation. LLM checks regex correctness, suggests canonical form, and assigns reasoning.
stages.llm_prompt_template: You are a regulatory rule engineer. Review this candidate rule extracted from an RBI document. Validate the regex pattern, suggest improvements, and confirm whether it generalises across future circulars. Return JSON: {candidate_id, validated_pattern, generalised: true/false, reasoning, recommended_file}

stages.name: confidence_threshold
stages.description: Only candidates with confidence >= 0.70 after LLM review proceed to approval.

stages.name: approval
stages.description: Candidate rules with status=approved are written to the recommended_file. Status updated to merged.

stages.name: regression_test
stages.description: After merge, re-run extraction on the source document. Verify the new rule improves obligation recall without introducing false positives.
